vllm.title: [Build] Only build 9.0a for scaled_mm and sparse kernels
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Only build 9.0a for scaled_mm and sparse CUTLASS kernels to save on build time and binary size (instead of 9.0 AND 9.0a). Since these kernels use 9.0a specific features and theres no pure 9.0 systems that we are aware of, only the 9.0a versions are getting used.
Also clean-up the cmake utility fixin

vllm.title: [V1][Metrics] Add initial Prometheus logger
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Part of #10582
Implement the vllm:num_requests_running and vllm:num_requests_waiting gauges from V0. This is a simple starting point from which to iterate towards parity with V0.
There's no need to use prometheus_client's "multi-processing mode" (at least at this stage) because these metrics all exi

vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR adds the most basic test for top-p & top-k sampling in V1. The newly added test does not check the correctness (or accuracy) though.

vllm.title: [Feature] [Spec decode]: Enable MLPSpeculator/Medusa and `prompt_logprobs` with ChunkedPrefill
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follow-up to #9291, an attempt at fixing prompt_logprobs and enabling hidden state-based speculators (MLP/Medusa).
The main issue with prompt_logprobs is that it changes the output of the mixed prefill-decode batch to have #prompt_tokens+#decode_tokens entries instead of just #sampling_entries (term

The PR fixes the issue reported in bug #12451
FIX #12451
Context:
Apparently, there is an issue with the nightly XLA 2.7 release that is incompatible with the upstream torch 2.7, causing vLLM to break on TPUs.
I also reported this issue on the XLA repo (pytorch/xla#8626)

vllm.title: [FEATURE] Enables offline /score for embedding models
vllm.author: gmarinho2
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Enables LLM.score() for all embedding models. The request_id consists of the request_ids of each embedding in the pair, separated by "_". The prompt_token_ids are the concatenation of all the token ids, in order and separated by the padding token when it is available. This PR is the first of two for
